name: USS Corpus Christi (PF-44)
events: 2 Aug 1944 HMAS Adelaide (A/Capt. L.E. Tozer, RAN), HMAS Dubbo (Lt.Cdr. W.J.C. Webb, RANR) and HMAS Horsham (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.H. Newby, RANR(S)), USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG) and USS Isabel (Lt. E.G. Zacher, USNR) conducted exercises off Fremantle with the submarines USS Hake (T/Cdr. F.E. Hayler, USN), USS Harder (T/Cdr. S.D. Dealey, USN), USS Jack (T/Cdr. A.E. Krapf, USN), USS Flier (T/Cdr. J.D. Crowley, USN) and USS Redfin (T/Cdr. M.H. Austin, USN). (2)

5 Aug 1944 HMAS Adelaide (A/Capt. L.E. Tozer, RAN) and HMAS Dubbo (Lt.Cdr. W.J.C. Webb, RANR) conducted radar tracking and gunnery exercises off Fremantle. They then joined USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG) and USS Isabel (Lt. E.G. Zacher, USNR) for exercises with the submarines USS Hake (T/Cdr. F.E. Hayler, USN) and USS Harder (T/Cdr. S.D. Dealey, USN). (2)

21 Dec 1944 During 21/22 December 1944, USS Besugo (T/Cdr. T.L. Wogan, USN), USS Blackfin (T/Lt.Cdr. C.L. Raguet, USN) and USS Hardhead (T/Cdr. F.A. Greenup, USN) conducted convoy attack exercises off Fremantle. These included night exercises.

The simulated convoy was made up of the frigate USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG) and the submarine rescue vessel USS Coucal (T/Lt. W.P. Baron, USN). They were escorted by the minesweepers HMAS Dubbo (T/Lt. F.W. Roberts, RANR(S)), HMAS Inverell (Lt. A.I. Chapman, RANR(S)), HMAS Mildura (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Little, RANR(S)), HMAS Warrnambool (Lt.Cdr.(Emgy.) E.J.B. Wight, RAN), armed yacht USS Isabel (Lt. E.G. Zacher, USNR), motor minesweepers USS YMS 11 (Lt. D.C. Maughan, USNR), USS YMS 72 (Lt. J.E. Reilly, USNR) and motor launches HMAS ML 815 (Lt. A.C. McAllister, RANVR) and HMAS ML 826 (Lt. S.A. Hancock, RANR).
Before commencement of the night exercises both motor minesweepers and both motor launches parted company. (3)

13 Feb 1945 USS Corpus Christi picks up 92 survivors from the American merchant Peter Silvester that was torpedoed and sunk on 6 February by German U-boat U-862 south-west of Australia in position 34°19'S, 99°37'E.
15 Mar 1945 Around 1500 H/15, USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN) departed from Fremantle for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.
Before proceeding on patrol exercises were carried out with USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG). These exercises were completed at 2200 H/15.

20 Mar 1945 Around 1300 H/20, USS Hardhead (T/Cdr. F.A. Greenup, USN) departed from Fremantle for her 4th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Gulf of Siam and to conduct a special (minelaying) operation.

Before proceeding on patrol exercises were carried out with USS Gabilan (T/Cdr. W.B. Parham, USN), USS Besugo (T/Lt.Cdr. H.E. Miller, USN), USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG), USS Hutchinson (Lt.Cdr. E.H. Houghtaling, USCG) and HMAS Kiama (T/Lt. S.J. Benson, RANR(S)).
On completion of the exercises around 2300 H/20, USS Gabilan and USS Hardhead proceeded on patrol. (4)

29 Jul 1945 Around 1430 H/29, USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) and USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN) departed from Fremantle for their 5th and 4th war patrols respectively. They were ordered to patrol in the South China Sea and Java Sea respectively.

On departure exercises were carried out with USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG) and USS SC-739 (Lt.(jg) H. Richardson, Jr., USNR) until 2200 H/29. The submarines then set course for Lombok Strait carrying out more exercises en-route. (5)
